⭐️ Newly minted #RecordingAcademy Lifetime Achievement Award honoree Shirley Caesar's (@pastorscaesar) career as the "First Lady of Gospel Music" has seen her take home Best Traditional Soul Gospel Album five times. 

🎶 One of those five trips to the podium was for her celebratory 1998 holiday album, 'Christmas With Shirley Caesar'. Featuring a balanced mix of traditional Christmas favorites and original holiday gospel songs, the album made for a triumphant follow-up to the 11-time GRAMMY winner's highly acclaimed previous holiday album, 1986's 'Christmasing'.

Did you know❓"Rudolph, The Red-Nosed Reindeer" was inducted into the GRAMMY Hall Of Fame in 1985. 

🎶 For Gene Autry, the singing cowboy who already had a massive film and singing career before Rudolph, this holiday diversion from his usual country repertoire became the biggest hit of his career.

#YearInReview 2️⃣0️⃣2️⃣1️⃣ 

👏 House Democrats passed the Build Back Better Act, inclusive of the Help Independent Tracks Succeed (HITS) Act, a bill we helped craft to aid and support artists and producers struggling with the ongoing economic fallout of the COVID-19 pandemic. 

👏 We extended membership invitations to 2,710 established music creators from wide-ranging backgrounds, genres, and disciplines. 

👏 Our @BlackMusicCollective and @AmazonMusic awarded the inaugural Your Future is Now scholarship, inclusive of a multi-year mentorship, to three students at HBCUs, offering them the opportunity to learn all facets of the music industry.

👏 For the second year, @MusiCares announced it will distribute aid to music professionals in need to cover essentials this holiday season via its Help for the Holidays program, offering $250 e-gift cards from Kroger and Walmart to the first 4,000 eligible applicants. 

👏 A historic $1.9 trillion package, the American Rescue Plan passed in Congress as a direct result of the advocacy of Recording Academy members who fought relentlessly for robust aid that will better benefit music makers and music small businesses. 

👏 Our GRAMMY Advocacy team hosted a series of “New Congress Webinars” to help past, present, & future music advocates understand the recent power shift in Washington, D.C. & what music creators can expect from the new Congress. 

👏 Academy members helped secure crucial creator-friendly COVID relief programs like the Paycheck Protection Program and Shuttered Venues Operators Grant and continue to work closely with lawmakers to ensure that future relief efforts contain robust aid for music makers and businesses. 

👏 The #GRAMMYs became the first major music awards show production to publicly commit to using an Inclusion Rider, designed to ensure equity and inclusion at every level during the production of the 64th GRAMMY Awards.

Did you know that Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. won a GRAMMY? 

🎙 The civil rights leader, who singlehandedly challenged ingrained racial barriers in the United States through peaceful protest, earned a number of accolades in his too-short life, including posthumously winning the GRAMMY for Best Spoken Word Recording for "Why I Oppose The War In Vietnam” in 1971.

👏 Not only that, but Dr. King was nominated two other times: once at the 11th GRAMMY Awards in 1969 for Best Spoken Word Recording “I Have A Dream”, and prior to that in 1964 at the 6th GRAMMY Awards, for Best Documentary, Spoken Word Or Drama Recording (Other Than Comedy) for "We Shall Overcome (The March On Washington...August 28, 1963)."

🖤 Today we celebrate and remember Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. and the significance of his efforts in advocating for civil rights and social justice.

I’m so pleased to let you know that the 64th Annual GRAMMY Awards have been rescheduled and will now be broadcast live from the MGM Grand Garden Arena in Las Vegas on Sunday, April 3 (8:00-11:30 PM, live ET/5:00-8:30 PM, live PT) on the CBS Television Network and will be available to stream live and on demand on Paramount+. Trevor Noah, the Emmy® Award-winning host of "The Daily Show", will return as master of ceremonies for Music’s Biggest Night®.
 
Additional details about the dates and locations of other official GRAMMY Week events, including the GRAMMY Awards Premiere Ceremony, MusiCares' Person of the Year, the Pre-GRAMMY Gala will be announced soon.
 
We are excited to take the GRAMMYs to Las Vegas for the very first time, and to put on a world class show.  From the moment we announced the postponement of the original show date, we have been inundated with heartfelt messages of support and solidarity from the artist community. We are humbled by their generosity and grateful for their unwavering commitment to the GRAMMY Awards and the Academy’s mission. We appreciate the leadership CBS and our production partners at Fulwell 73 have shown during these challenging weeks and the flexibility of everyone who worked toward this solution.
 
Onward,
Harvey Mason jr.

We are thrilled to announce our partnership with @glaad in 2022 to foster LGBTQ+ inclusion in the music community, including initiatives such as:

👏 Editorial content spotlighting LGBTQ+ music artists on GRAMMY.com and GLAAD.org, as well as each organization's social media channels.
👏 Support from the Recording Academy for the 33rd Annual GLAAD Media Awards in Los Angeles on Sat, April 2, 2022 and New York City on Fri, May 6, 2022.
👏 LGBTQ+-focused DEI training sessions hosted by the GLAAD Media Institute for Recording Academy elected leaders and staff to learn more about LGBTQ+ people and issues.
👏 Participation from key GLAAD executives in the #RecordingAcademy's DEI speakers series throughout the upcoming year.

🎶 #MardiGras music is a reflection of southwestern Louisiana, a cultural pocket of African American, European and Caribbean traditions. The regional sounds that sprung from immigration, slavery, military conflicts, commerce, faith, and — most of all — entertainment, occupy a unique and deeply influential space in American music. 

🎧 Click the link in our bio to learn how #TromboneShorty, #DrJohn, #TheWildMagnolias, and others soundtrack the #NewOrleans celebration and indulge in a playlist of festival favorites.

It's proof that the ongoing fight for equality and equity is as inspirational and urgent as ever.

👏 During our @blackmusiccollective #BMCHonors GRAMMY Week event, we will be honoring 12-time GRAMMY-winning artist #JohnLegend with our first-ever Global Impact Award. 

⭐️ As a 33-time GRAMMY-nominated singer, songwriter, producer, and philanthropist, John Legend began his musical career behind the scenes over 20 years ago working with artists such as #LaurynHill, #JayZ and #AliciaKeys. He then signed to #KanyeWest's GOOD Music and released his debut album 'Get Lifted', which reached the top 10 on the Billboard 200 Charts and was certified double platinum by the RIAA. 

🎶 Legend made history as the first Black man to win the coveted EGOT (Emmy, GRAMMY, Oscar, and Tony Award), and is currently a part of the @RecordingAcad’s Board of Trustees and is an Honorary Chair of the #BlackMusicCollective.

🎵 Legend will be celebrated with a performance by @SummerWalker, and @DNice will be spinning music throughout the night, with @MCLyte as the voice of the evening, @AdamBlackstone as the musical director and Jimmy Jam (@flytetymejam), the first Black Chair of the Recording Academy Board of Trustees and Songwriting Hall of Fame inductee, will be making remarks.

A trailblazer designed the @RecordingAcademy Global Impact Award for #BMCHonors. @SheronBarer created a masterpiece for our culture and vision that represents legacy, love and solidarity.  
 
▪️The petals of the gramophone flower are sculpted utilizing upcycled shattered glass which he melted down, colored and re-blown. 
 
▪️ The stem and stamen are constructed of ammunition that I sourced from war sites which I also melted down and sculpted then dipped in 24 Karat Gold. 
 
▪️ The first cubic layer of the platform is made of glass. 
 
▪️ The base of the platform is cast of reformed war ammunition then plated in black nickel.

During this year’s #GRAMMYs, we became the first-ever major award show to implement an Inclusion Rider. We recognize that advocating for change at-large first requires enacting change from within. This year during GRAMMY Week, we focused on three areas:

👏 A commitment beyond compliance for the Disability Culture and accessibility for our viewers
👏 The women’s community 
👏 The LGBTQIA community

At the #RecordingAcademy, we have the power to help move the industry forward in how it includes and supports creators with disabilities. We are committed to going above and beyond simple compliance, working for creators and music professionals with disabilities by building stronger, more inclusive communities and providing access to resources. 

This year, we worked closely with Amber G (@ambergproductions) who brought in a diverse team of interpreters to welcome our deaf and hard of hearing community to this year’s #GRAMMYs. Alongside @colorofchange, we worked together to see diversity and inclusion happen, inspiring change for future generations. 

Image description: Amber G (woman wearing back with pink hair) signing backstage at the GRAMMY Awards. Video footage of the GRAMMY red carpet and disability accessible areas inside the GRAMMY Awards.

Matt (with a smile) and Martise (Deafinatley Dope Representative) helped us take a step in the right direction as we continue to work to make this a movement and come together to give access to the hearing community during the this year’s #GRAMMYs. 

In addition to our on-site accessibility, we also implemented tools across our social platforms such as burn-in captions, alt text, and camel casing hashtags to ensure that Blind, Deaf, Hard of Hearing, and additional members of the Disabled community could join the celebration of Music’s Biggest Night. 

We’re looking forward to further enriching our commitment and exploring new opportunities to achieve inclusion and access for all in the music community.

While #Biggie is no longer with us, we continue celebrating the iconic rapper’s legacy within #HipHop and the larger music community. 

With the talent he carried at a young age, Biggie’s career began early and was recognized by #Diddy who signed him to his label. 

It wasn’t until 1994 when Biggie’s career took off with his debut album, ‘Ready to Die’, which went on to give us one of his most recognized songs, “Big Poppa”. 

Following his debut success, Biggie’s career continued in 1997 with the success of his posthumous GRAMMY-nominated album, ‘Life After Death’, giving us the GRAMMY-nominated songs, “Hypnotize” and “Mo Money Mo Problems”. 

While Biggie wasn’t able to see his impact, his influence and musical legacy within #HipHop continues to this day. 🎶

👏 65 years ago today the #RecordingAcademy was born to represent the voices of performers, songwriters, producers, engineers, and all music professionals.

What began as a meeting between the Founder's Committee — a group of record executives — in 1957 has now blossomed into an Academy that
✨ Advocates on behalf of music creators
✨ Celebrates artistic excellence through the #GRAMMYs
✨ Supports music people in times of need through MusiCares Foundation
✨ Invests in its future through the GRAMMY Museum, #GRAMMYU, #BlackMusicCollective, and our internship program.

As we look back today, we also look forward and remain committed to ensuring we represent the diverse music community that makes up the music industry at large.

📲 Read more about the Recording Academy's beginnings: grm.my/3GpiaVC

Taking a trip down memory lane and remembering some #GRAMMYs moments over the years in honor of us turning 65! ✨ 

🎶 #RobertaFlack wins Record Of The Year for a second year in a row at the 16th GRAMMY Awards in 1974 
🎶 #Selena wins GRAMMY for Best Mexican-American Album at the 36th GRAMMY Awards in 1994 
🎶 #LaurynHill wins five GRAMMY Awards in 1998, including Album Of The Year 
🎶 #RickyMartin performs “La Copa De La Vida” at the 41st GRAMMY Awards in 1999, introducing him to a wider audience

🎶 #EltonJohn and #Eminem perform together in 2001 at the 43rd GRAMMY Awards 
🎶 #TinaTurner returns to the stage with #Beyoncé to perform “Proud Mary” at the 50th GRAMMY Awards in 2008 
🎶 #Adele takes home 6 GRAMMY Awards in 2012, tying Beyoncé's record for most GRAMMY Awards won 

🎶 #AliciaKeys, #LadyGaga, #JenniferLopez, #MichelleObama and #JadaPinkettSmith open the 61st GRAMMY Awards in 2019 
🎶 #HarryStyles performs at the GRAMMYs for the first time in 2021, singing "Watermelon Sugar" and wins his first GRAMMY Award for Best Pop Vocal Album 
🎶 #JonBatiste wins Album Of The Year at the 64th GRAMMY Awards this year and gives a heartfelt speech

💜 #Prince’s legacy lives on forever. 

✨ For his debut album, 1978's 'For You,' he wrote, produced, arranged, composed, and played all 27 instruments used on the album. 

🎶 It was Prince's fifth studio album 1982's '1999', that helped him take off. It was his first to dent the Top 10, with his iconic song "Little Red Corvette" peaking at No. 6. In 1984 Prince earned his first three career #GRAMMYs and made his stage debut, performing "Baby, I'm A Star." 

✨ In 2017, the seven-time GRAMMY-winner's 1987 album, 'Sign O' The Times,' was inducted into the GRAMMY Hall Of Fame where he will forever be celebrated as the legendary musician he was.

In honor of #DisabilityPrideMonth, we invited Recording Artists and Music Professionals with Disabilities (RAMPD) to create an article with tips on how the music industry can support creators and creative professionals who live with disabilities.

✨#RAMPD is a coalition of established creators with disabilities who work to promote equitable inclusion, visibility and accessibility in the music industry.

📲 Click the link in our bio to learn about key ways the music industry can support creators and creative professionals with disabilities on a year-round basis and in a meaningful manner.
